question: What lines make up the RCS piping outside the RPV?

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear identified as the most likely degradation mechanism and secondary corrosion risks considered low.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency valves, and associated piping. It outlines the integration of these components within the reactor vessel and specifies the boundaries between the primary and secondary sides of the steam generator system, emphasizing the RPV’s role as the main pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies the use of nondestructive examinations, controlled shipping environments, and quality assurance measures to ensure the integrity and safety of the reactor vessel. Key references to industry codes and standards are also provided.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for removing noncondensible gases and maintaining pressure boundary integrity to prevent radionuclide release. The RCS interfaces with several other systems, including emergency core cooling and chemical control, to ensure safe and efficient operation.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes the main components of the NuScale reactor system, focusing on the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping, and the Pressurizer (PZR). The RPV serves as a primary barrier to fission product release and houses key reactor internals, while the RCS piping connects essential systems and the PZR is integrated within the RPV. Each component's functions and connections are briefly outlined, with references to further detailed sections.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale reactor pressurizer, specifying the alloys and standards for each component, such as the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, and bolting. It emphasizes the use of corrosion-resistant materials compliant with ASME BPVC Section II specifications to ensure compatibility and durability in the reactor coolant system.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) contains two steam generators (SGs) integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer and safety valves. The steam generator system (SGS) includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the RPV itself forming the outer pressure boundary for the SGs. The design ensures separation between the primary and secondary sides, with specific components forming the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for materials and components used in reactor systems.

edges.description: Section B lists specific lines such as the degasification line and reactor vent valves that are part of the RCS piping outside the RPV, while Section A discusses general RCS piping design and degradation mechanisms, thus Section B provides details that specify which lines are included in the context of Section A's general description.

Section A discusses the design and degradation mechanisms of Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping, while Section B lists specific system components and operational procedures, together providing information necessary to identify which piping lines constitute the RCS outside the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV).

Section A lists specific RCS piping lines (injection, discharge, pressurizer spray, high-point degasification vent) located outside the RPV, while Section B describes the function and operation of some of these lines (e.g., degasification line, reactor vent valves) in the context of RCS operation.

Section A lists the RCS piping lines outside the RPV, such as injection, discharge, pressurizer spray, and high-point degasification vent lines, while Section B describes operational use of the high-point degasification line, thus providing a specific example of a line mentioned in Section A.

Section B describes specific system components and operational lines (such as the RPV high point degasification line) that are part of the RCS piping outside the RPV, which directly addresses the analysis question, while Section A does not provide this information.

Section A describes the function and purpose of the RCS degasification line and reactor vent valves outside the RPV, while Section B specifies the operational steps and components (such as the degasification line and venting process) that make up and utilize these lines, thus providing details for the general description in Section A.

Section A references specific components and materials of the pressurizer and their locations relative to the RPV, while Section B discusses the design and operational considerations of the reactor coolant system (RCS) piping, both contributing information necessary to identify which lines constitute the RCS piping outside the RPV.

Section B lists the RCS injection, discharge, pressurizer spray, and high-point degasification vent lines as components outside the RPV, while Section A provides detailed descriptions of the pressurizer spray nozzles and their locations, connecting the general system layout in B to specific line components in A.

Section A references the material specifications and component descriptions of the RPV upper shell and upper head, which are further detailed in Section B's discussion of the RPV's inspection, shipment, and handling requirements, thus connecting the design and material aspects of RCS piping outside the RPV to the regulatory and quality assurance requirements for those same components.

Section A lists the reactor coolant system (RCS) injection, discharge, pressurizer spray, and high-point degasification vent lines as components associated with the RCS outside the RPV, while Section B discusses the design and operational considerations of RCS piping, specifically focusing on steam generator (SG) tubing, thus Section A provides the specific line types that Section B's general discussion of RCS piping encompasses.

Section A and Section B are identical excerpts listing the components and lines contained within the RPV, including the RCS injection, discharge, pressurizer spray, and high-point degasification vent lines, which are relevant to identifying the RCS piping outside the RPV.

Section B lists the RCS piping lines outside the RPV, which Section A references in its discussion of pressurizer spray nozzles and their locations, connecting the specific components described in A to the broader system layout in B.
Section A provides references and standards that underpin the technical and regulatory basis for the design and integrity of the RCS piping described in Section B.

Section A provides references and standards that underpin the technical and regulatory basis for the RCS piping design described in Section B, which details the specific lines making up the RCS outside the RPV.

Section A and Section B are related because both reference the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report and ASME NQA-1-2008/1a-2009 Addenda, indicating that the quality assurance requirements for RCS piping outside the RPV (Section A) are also applicable to the shipment and installation of the RPV (Section B).
